name: song-builder-personalize
note: M-13 g3 PERSONALIZED SONG BUILDER: the section-template suggestion panel now CONSUMES the local competency profile (music.competency.v1) - a re-rank, never a filter, plus a visible one-line why-cue (Songbook.personalizeSuggestions). Seed a HIGH comp-progressions level (70) via localStorage before opening the builder (an init step, since competency data is read fresh on every render - no page-boot ordering to fight). Build a Verse (C-F-G), Add as Verse, Clear the canvas so the SONG tray offers Verse template chips. Against the REAL catalog (song-templates.js mining songs.json), the top-ranked Verse suggestion WITHOUT a profile is the plain I-V-vi-IV loop (From With or Without You) - with a level-70 profile it's the more varied i-bVI-bIII-bVII minor-pop loop (Africa/Zombie/Stay With Me), tagged 'adds variety'. Tap it to accept -> fills the progression through the normal chord-add path AND records comp-progressions evidence (the compounding loop: accepted suggestions feed the next session's ranking). RED-FIRST vs unmodified main: personalizeSuggestions/_tag/_boost do not exist there, so the first chip's roman stays I-V-vi-IV even under a seeded high-skill profile and step 'the personalized pick now leads' fails.

steps.label: seed a high comp-progressions competency profile (level 70, no preferences)
one buffered Verse, progression NOT cleared (A3)
progression empty, tray still visible (buffer non-empty)
Verse label is selected (default) - suggestions are for the section we're building next
the PERSONALIZED pick now leads: first chip is the varied minor-pop loop, not the plain default
the why-cue names it: the first chip's provenance line carries 'adds variety'
accepting the chip fills the progression through the normal chord-add path
the compounding loop: accepting a suggestion recorded comp-progressions evidence back to the profile
